IIT Bhilai Launches Three-Day Teacher Training Programme to Strengthen Skill-Based Education
articles.full_content:
Teacher Empowerment Focus: IIT Bhilai Inaugurates Intensive Training Programme for 400 Educators
Indian Institute of Technology Bhilai today inaugurated a three-day Teacher Training Programme sponsored by Samagra Shiksha, aimed at strengthening pedagogical practices, promoting skill-oriented education and encouraging value-based learning among school teachers. The programme will train around 400 teachers in eight batches, with each batch undergoing an intensive three-day training at the institute.
The inaugural session was graced by Professor Rajiv Prakash, Director, Indian Institute of Technology Bhilai, who served as the Patron of the programme and Chief Guest. In his address, Professor Prakash underscored the pivotal role of teachers in nation-building and in shaping an enlightened and responsible society. He urged educators to embrace self-reliance, focus on continuous skill development and guide students towards strong character formation rooted in ethical and value-based education.
The programme is being convened by Dr Mehboob Alam and Dr Dhruv Pratap Singh. Addressing the participants, Dr Mehboob Alam described teachers as guiding lights of society who work tirelessly to dispel the darkness of illiteracy and ignorance. Dr Dhruv Pratap Singh highlighted the enduring impact of teachers on students’ lives, citing former President Dr Avul Pakir Jainulabdeen Abdul Kalam as an example of a global personality who consistently acknowledged the contribution of his school teachers in shaping his success.
The inaugural ceremony was also attended by Dr Kuldeep Kumar Kataria, Dean of Academic Affairs, along with Dr Mahavir Sharma, Head of the Department of Physics, and Dr Mayilmurugan, Head of the Department of Chemistry, as well as faculty members and participating teachers. The programme is expected to significantly enhance teaching competencies, encourage innovative classroom practices and reinforce the integration of skill development in school education across the region.
articles.summary: IIT Bhilai Inaugurates Intensive Training Programme for 400 Educators Indian Institute of Technology Bhilai today inaugurated a three-day Teacher Training Programme sponsored by Samagra Shiksha, aimed at strengthening pedagogical practices, promoting skill-oriented education and encouraging value-based learning among school teachers. The programme will train around 400 teachers in eight batches, with each batch undergoing an intensive three-day training at the institute. The inaugural session was graced by Professor Rajiv Prakash, Director, Indian Institute of Technology Bhilai, who served as the Patron of the programme and Chief Guest.

Ethanol Blending in India
articles.full_content:
Policy Evolution, Key Milestones and Frequently Raised Concerns
Overview of the Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) Programme
Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) Programme has emerged as a key pillar of India's energy transition and biofuel strategy. It aims to improve energy security, support farmers, and reduce environmental impacts. The programme also promotes greater use of domestically produced renewable fuel.
Under the EBP Programme, ethanol blending has increased from less than 1.5 percent in 2013-14 to 20 percent in 2025-26. India achieved the 20 percent blending target five years ahead of schedule. Ethanol procurement rose from about 38 crore litres in Ethanol Supply Year (ESY) 2013-14 to over 1,200 crore litres (projected) in 2025-26. Production capacity expanded nearly fivefold from 421 crore litres in 2014 to about 2,000 crore litres in 2026. This expansion has reduced crude oil imports and saved valuable foreign exchange. It has also lowered greenhouse gas emissions and strengthened farmers' incomes through new market opportunities.
Strengthening Energy Security through Ethanol Blending
India imports close to 88.5 percent of the crude oil it consumes. That single number explains a great deal about why ethanol blending sits so high on the policy agenda. Every barrel of oil bought from abroad exposes the country to price swings and supply shocks that are entirely outside its control. Ethanol produced from Indian sugarcane, maize and rice offers a way to blunt that exposure using resources grown at home.
Since the ESY 2014-15 (up to May 2026), the programme has delivered results that go well beyond a policy target on paper.
₹ 1.90+ lakh crore
Saved in foreign exchange since 2014-15
Crude oil substituted
CO 2 emissions cut
₹ 1.60+ lakh crore
Additional farmer earnings
*MT – Metric Tonnes
Setting the Record Straight
Here is what the evidence actually shows on the key concerns surrounding ethanol blending, along with the more colourful myths that have circulated on social media.
E20 cuts mileage by 30 percent.
The 30 percent figure refers only to ethanol's lower calorific value compared to petrol, not to a drop-in real-world mileage. Mileage depends on driving habits, tyre pressure, servicing and AC load far more than fuel type.
E20 damages engines, especially older ones.
No widespread pattern of engine failure linked to E20 has been reported since rollout. E20 was cleared only after extensive testing by SIAM, ARAI and IOCL together with vehicle makers.
Ethanol is not a high-performance fuel and reduces vehicle performance.
Ethanol is a high-octane fuel, with a research octane number of about 108.5, compared to 84.4 for petrol. E20 raises the effective octane rating of Indian petrol to around 95, improving combustion in modern engines. Vehicles calibrated for E20 can deliver better acceleration, smoother performance, and lower emissions.
Insurance companies refuse claims for E20 related damage.
Insurers and OEMs have clarified: E20 has NO impact on insu
articles.summary: Policy Evolution, Key Milestones and Frequently Raised Concerns Overview of the Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) Programme Ethanol Blended Petrol (EBP) Programme has emerged as a key pillar of India's energy transition and biofuel strategy. It aims to improve energy security, support farmers, and reduce environmental impacts. The programme also promotes greater use of domestically produced renewable fuel.

NSTI (Women), Noida Opens Admissions for NCVET Approved CTS Skill Courses for Academic Session 2026–27
articles.full_content:
The National Skill Training Institute (NSTI) for Women -NSTI(W), Sector-1, Noida, under the Directorate General of Training (DGT), Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE), Government of India, has invited applications for admission to its NCVET approved (NSQF Aligned) skill development programmes under the Craftsmen Training Scheme (CTS) for the 2026–27 academic session.
Interested candidates can check the detailed eligibility criteria, available trades, and other admission guidelines, and submit their applications online through the institute's admission portal at https://nstiwnoida.dgtmsde.in by selecting the CTS Online Registration link for the 2026–27 academic session. The last date for submission of onli ne applications is 12 July, 2026 until 06:00pm.
As a premier institution dedicated to empowering women through quality vocational education, NSTI(W) Noida offers industry-oriented training in a wide range of conventional and emerging sectors, including Artificial Intelligence, Drone Technology, Semiconductor Technology, 5G Networks, Fashion Design, Computer Applications, Computer Aided Embroidery & Designing, Interior Design, Cosmetology, and Front Office Operations, among others.
These programmes are aligned with the National Skills Qualifications Framework (NSQF), enabling trainees to pursue higher education and access various employment opportunities.
Committed to the vision of the Skill India Mission, NSTI(W), Noida is empowering women with industry-aligned skills, nurturing future-ready talent, and contributing to India's emergence as the Skill Capital of the world.
articles.summary: The National Skill Training Institute (NSTI) for Women -NSTI(W), Sector-1, Noida, under the Directorate General of Training (DGT), Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE), Government of India, has invited applications for admission to its NCVET approved (NSQF Aligned) skill development programmes under the Craftsmen Training Scheme (CTS) for the 2026–27 academic session. Interested candidates can check the detailed eligibility criteria, available trades, and other admission guidelines, and submit their applications online through the institute's admission portal at https://nstiwnoida.dgtmsde.in by selecting the CTS Online Registration link for the 2026–27 academic session. The last date for submission of onli ne applications is 12 July, 2026 until 06:00pm.

ARAI Reaffirms Rigorous Validation Standards for E20 Ethanol-Blended Fuel and Vehicle Performance
articles.full_content:
Comprehensive testing of 40,000–60,000 kms confirms minimal fuel efficiency impact and ensures long-term engine health - Dr. Reji Mathai
Pune, July 4, 2026
The Automotive Research Association of India (ARAI) today hosted a press conference at its Corporate Headquarters in Kothrud, Pune, to share critical updates and technical clarifications regarding the adoption of E20 (20% ethanol-blended) as an automotive fuel in India.
Addressing members of the media, Dr. Reji Mathai, Director of ARAI, alongside senior ARAI officials, addressed key queries surrounding E20 fuel readiness, vehicle durability, and consumer performance expectations. The panel reiterated that all E20-compatible vehicles undergo stringent, structured validation and verification procedures before being introduced to the commercial market. These processes meet international standards as well as the customized testing standards of Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs).
“Validation and verification of E20 fuel for vehicles are subjected to extensive durability, performance, and reliability tests under various operating conditions to ensure seamless performance and long-term engine health," the panel emphasized. "These evaluations assess the impact of ethanol blending on critical vehicle systems, including the engine, fuel lines, and other components, ensuring compliance with quality and safety standards."
The implementation of BS VI vehicles compatible with E20 fuel represents a monumental step toward building a sustainable and self-reliant supply chain for India. To achieve this, vehicles across different categories have been tested by OEMs through rigorous field and laboratory testing regimes spanning approximately 40,000 to 60,000 kilometers.
Minimal Efficiency Impact:
• Standard laboratory tests carried out on validated vehicles indicate that the fuel efficiency drop associated with E20 fuel is minimal, staying within a narrow range of 2% to 6%
• Any variations in real-world fuel consumption and overall efficiency are driven by external operational factors, including driver behavior, road conditions, and the baseline condition of the vehicle under test.
• Maintenance Guidelines: In alignment with standard prevailing practices and OEM advice, consumers are encouraged to take their vehicles to their respective authorized dealers for regular maintenance and servicing to address any operational issues.
• The panel concluded by reassuring the public that E20-ready vehicles are developed and validated through comprehensive engineering processes designed to maintain absolute reliability and provide a safe and dependable ownership experience for Indian consumers.
• The automotive industry remains fully committed to supporting India's ethanol-blending roadmap while ensuring that customers receive vehicles that meet stringent quality benchmarks.
Harshal Akude/Parshuram Kor
Follow us on social media: @PIBMumbai / PIBMumbai /pibmumbai pibmumbai[at]gmail[dot]com /PIBMumbai /pibmumbai
articles.summary: Comprehensive testing of 40,000–60,000 kms confirms minimal fuel efficiency impact and ensures long-term engine health - Dr. Reji Mathai Pune, July 4, 2026 The Automotive Research Association of India (ARAI) today hosted a press conference at its Corporate Headquarters in Kothrud, Pune, to share critical updates and technical clarifications regarding the adoption of E20 (20% ethanol-blended) as an automotive fuel in India. Reji Mathai, Director of ARAI, alongside senior ARAI officials, addressed key queries surrounding E20 fuel readiness, vehicle durability, and consumer performance expectations.
